test_cases.test_case: Password just below minimum length (7 chars) is rejected by validation

test_cases.expected_result: FAIL: Client- or server-side validation prevents authentication because password violates the "minimum length 8" constraint. The system should present an error (do not proceed to successful sign-in).

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click Sign In with incorrect credentials results in single failure flow and password cleared once

test_cases.expected_result: The UI should follow the described failure behavior exactly once: show the message "Incorrect email or password. Please try again", clear the Password field, and allow another attempt. There should be a single visible failure message/indication (no duplicate messages) and the password input must be emptied.

test_cases.test_case: Path A - Complete data: multiple accounts display correctly with mask, badge, footer and ordering
preconditions: Three customer accounts exist with Account Numbers, Account Types, Current Balances, Account Status = Active, and distinct Open Dates (earliest, middle, latest)
steps: Navigate to the Accounts Overview dashboard
Observe the accounts table and its rows
test_cases.expected_result: Table displays three rows. Each row shows Account Number as a clickable link (rendered as link element) but masked to show only last 4 digits (per AST constraint 'mask: show only last 4 digits as ****5001'), Account Type, Current Balance, Account Status shown as an 'Active' badge, and Open Date. Footer contains a 'Total Balance' row that equals the sum of the three Current Balance values. Rows are ordered by Open Date ascending (earliest first) matching default_sort/Open Date asc and row_ordering earliest_first_by_creation_date in the AST.

test_cases.test_case: Path B - Minimum viable: single account row displays required columns and footer
preconditions: Single customer account exists with Account Number, Account Type, Current Balance, Account Status = Active, and Open Date
steps: Navigate to the Accounts Overview dashboard
View the accounts table with one row
test_cases.expected_result: Table shows one row with Account Number displayed as a masked link (only last 4 digits visible), Account Type, Current Balance, Account Status shown as 'Active' badge, and Open Date. Footer shows 'Total Balance' equal to the single row's Current Balance.

test_cases.test_case: Path C - Alternative route: click Account Number link and verify UI remains on Accounts Overview
preconditions: At least one customer account exists
steps: Navigate to the Accounts Overview dashboard
Click the Account Number link in the first row
test_cases.expected_result: Account Number is rendered as a clickable link (AST interactive: true). Clicking the link does not navigate away from the Accounts Overview (description: clickable but not implemented); the dashboard welcome message and accounts table remain visible and unchanged.

test_cases.test_case: Row action presence: 'Open Account' row action is visible and disabled

test_cases.test_case: Masking verification: account number displays only last 4 digits in masked format

steps: Navigate to the Accounts Overview dashboard
Look at the Account Number cell for the account whose number ends with '5001'
test_cases.expected_result: The Account Number cell shows the masked value using only the last 4 digits, e.g. '****5001', consistent with the AST constraint 'mask: show only last 4 digits as ****5001'.

test_cases.test_case: Footer total balance sum verification with multiple balances

steps: Navigate to the Accounts Overview dashboard
Scroll to the table footer and read the Total Balance row
test_cases.expected_result: Footer contains a row labeled 'Total Balance' and displays the sum of the Current Balance column (100.00 + 200.00 + 300.00 = 600.00) as specified by the footer_rows operation 'sum' in the AST.

test_cases.test_case: Default sort and row ordering by Open Date ascending

steps: Reveal the row actions for any account row

test_cases.test_case: Footer 'Total Balance' correctly sums positive and negative balances to zero
preconditions: Dashboard loaded showing Accounts_Table with footer row 'Total Balance' (AST footer_rows operation: sum on Current Balance)
Two account rows exist: Current Balance = 100.00 and Current Balance = -100.00
test_cases.expected_result: PASS: The footer sum must reflect the sum across all accounts per AST footer_rows: the total balance displayed equals $0.00 (100.00 + -100.00 = 0.00).

test_cases.test_case: Empty accounts list displays the footer row and a total balance of zero
preconditions: Dashboard loaded showing Accounts_Table (per description 'table includes a footer row displaying the total balance across all accounts')
test_cases.expected_result: PASS: The footer row must still be present and display the total balance across all accounts. With zero accounts the total must be $0.00 (or explicitly show zero), consistent with 'total balance across all accounts'.

test_cases.test_case: Row ordering must follow creation date (earliest first) even when Open Date sort would produce a different order
preconditions: AST: row_ordering = 'earliest_first_by_creation_date' and default_sort.column = 'Open Date' direction = 'asc'
Two accounts exist: Account A has creation_date = 2020-01-01 and Open Date = 2021-01-01; Account B has creation_date = 2021-06-01 and Open Date = 2020-01-01
steps: Observe the order of rows in the table from top (first) to bottom (last)
test_cases.expected_result: PASS: Per AST row_ordering, rows are ordered by account creation date earliest first. Account A (creation_date 2020-01-01) must appear before Account B, despite Account B having an earlier Open Date. The creation_date ordering takes precedence so the top row is Account A.

test_cases.test_case: When creation dates are identical, default_sort by Open Date ascending is applied as a secondary ordering
preconditions: AST: row_ordering = 'earliest_first_by_creation_date' and default_sort.column = 'Open Date' direction = 'asc'
Two accounts exist with identical creation_date = 2020-01-01 but different Open Dates: Account X Open Date = 2020-02-01, Account Y Open Date = 2020-01-01
steps: Observe the order of the two rows with identical creation_date
test_cases.expected_result: The table must order rows by creation date first (they tie) and then apply the default_sort on Open Date ascending. Therefore Account Y (Open Date 2020-01-01) should appear above Account X (Open Date 2020-02-01).

test_cases.expected_result: PASS: The description does not state a prohibition on reusing the current password. If the system enforces only the described checks (verification, strong-password policy, and match) and the password satisfies the strong-password policy, the system should update credentials and display "Password changed successfully." If the system has an additional (not-specified) policy disallowing reuse, it should instead show a validation error highlighting New Password — such a rejection is outside the current spec.

test_cases.test_case: Collapsing and reopening the panel preserves entered form values (no loss on collapse)
preconditions: User is on the Security Settings page
Collapsible panel containing the Change Password form is expanded
steps: Enter values into Current Password, New Password, and Confirm New Password fields (do not submit)
test_cases.expected_result: PASS: After reopening the panel the form fields should retain the values the user entered (no unexpected clearing). If fields are cleared, it should be considered a UI-state loss bug (the AST identifies the form as being inside a collapsible panel).

test_cases.test_case: Pasting Confirm_New_Password with a trailing newline causes mismatch and validation
preconditions: User is on the Security Settings page
User knows their correct current password
steps: Enter correct value into Current Password
Paste into Confirm New Password the same visible text but with an extra trailing newline character (e.g., "Str0ng!Pass\n")
test_cases.expected_result: FAIL: Confirm New Password should be treated as not matching New Password; validation errors highlight Confirm New Password indicating it "must match New_Password". No "Password changed successfully." message appears.

test_cases.test_case: Secure Message — Path A: Complete data (subject, category, body, allowed attachment)
steps: Enter Subject: 'Unable to access billing details - account XYZ'
Enter Message Body (rich text): 'Detailed description of the billing access issue and steps already taken.'
Attach a valid file type (e.g., 'screenshot.png') using the Attachment control
test_cases.expected_result: Message sent successfully with ticket ID

test_cases.test_case: Secure Message — Path B: Minimum viable (only required field Message Body)
steps: Enter Message Body (rich text): 'Short message describing the issue.'
test_cases.expected_result: Message sent successfully with ticket ID

test_cases.test_case: Secure Message — Path C: Alternative route (different Category, no Subject, no attachment)
steps: Enter Message Body (rich text): 'Reporting a technical error observed on the dashboard.'
test_cases.expected_result: Message sent successfully with ticket ID

test_cases.test_case: Schedule Callback — Path A: Complete data (reason, preferred next-business-day date, time window, edited prefilled phone)
steps: Select a Reason for Call option (choose any available option)
Set Preferred Date to the next business day or later per system rules
Enter Preferred Time Window: '10:00 AM - 12:00 PM'
Verify Phone Number is prefilled, optionally edit it to a valid format (e.g., '555-123-4567')
test_cases.expected_result: Callback request submitted; sends email confirmation

test_cases.test_case: Schedule Callback — Path B: Minimum viable (only required fields Preferred Date and Phone Number)
steps: Set Preferred Date to the next business day or later per system rules
Ensure Phone Number is present (use prefilled value) and in valid format
test_cases.expected_result: Callback request submitted; sends email confirmation

test_cases.test_case: Schedule Callback — Path C: Alternative route (edit prefilled phone number and choose a later valid date)
steps: Select a Reason for Call option (if available)
Set Preferred Date to a valid business day later than the next business day
Leave Preferred Time Window blank or enter a different time window than Path A
Edit the prefilled Phone Number to a different valid phone number (e.g., '555-987-6543')
test_cases.expected_result: Callback request submitted; sends email confirmation

test_cases.test_case: Category dropdown options verification (Account, Technical, Security, Other are present and selectable)
steps: Verify the dropdown lists the options: 'Account', 'Technical', 'Security', 'Other'
Select each option in turn and confirm the selection is applied
test_cases.expected_result: Category dropdown shows and allows selection of Account, Technical, Security, Other

test_cases.test_case: Schedule Callback — Phone Number pre-filled verification and editability
steps: Observe the Phone Number field on form load
Confirm the Phone Number field contains the user's phone number (pre-filled)
Attempt to edit the Phone Number field and change it to a valid alternate number
test_cases.expected_result: Phone Number field is pre-filled with the user's number and is editable

test_cases.test_case: Send Message with missing required Message Body
preconditions: User is on the Support Center page
steps: In Secure Message form, leave Message Body empty
Optionally fill Subject and Category or leave them blank
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error displayed for Message Body indicating it is required (inline guidance shown for Message Body)

test_cases.test_case: Request Callback with all required fields empty (clear prefilled phone and leave date empty)
preconditions: User is on the Support Center page
steps: In Schedule Callback form, clear the prefilled Phone Number field
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors displayed inline for Preferred Date and Phone Number indicating they are required

test_cases.test_case: Request Callback with Preferred Date earlier than next business day (violates business-rule constraint)
preconditions: User is on the Support Center page
steps: In Schedule Callback form, set Preferred Date to today (not the next business day)
Ensure Phone Number field has a valid phone number
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error displayed for Preferred Date indicating it must be at least the next business day

test_cases.test_case: Request Callback with invalid Preferred Date format
preconditions: User is on the Support Center page
steps: In Schedule Callback form, enter an invalid date string into Preferred Date (e.g., '99/99/9999')
Ensure Phone Number field has a valid phone number
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error displayed for Preferred Date indicating the date is invalid/incorrect format

test_cases.test_case: Request Callback with invalid Phone Number format
preconditions: User is on the Support Center page
steps: In Schedule Callback form, edit Phone Number to an invalid value (e.g., 'abc123')
Set Preferred Date to a valid next-business-day date
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error displayed for Phone Number indicating it must match phone number format

test_cases.test_case: Send Message with Subject exceeding system length limits
preconditions: User is on the Support Center page
steps: In Secure Message form, enter a Subject longer than allowed by the system (very long string)
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation guidance displayed for Subject indicating it does not meet subject length limits

test_cases.test_case: Send Message with disallowed attachment type
preconditions: User is on the Support Center page
steps: In Secure Message form, attach a disallowed file type (e.g., .exe or other blocked extension)
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation guidance displayed for Attachment indicating the file type is not allowed

test_cases.test_case: Preferred Date = next business day (should PASS)
preconditions: User is on Support Center page
Phone Number prefilled with a syntactically-valid number
steps: Set Preferred Date to the next business day (skip weekends; if today is Wednesday, pick Thursday; if today is Friday, pick next Monday)
Optionally set Reason for Call and Preferred Time Window
Leave Phone Number as prefilled or ensure it remains valid
test_cases.expected_result: PASS — Submission succeeds and UI shows the success text 'Callback request submitted.'; no inline validation error about the date is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Preferred Date = today (should FAIL)
preconditions: User is on Support Center page
steps: Set Preferred Date to today (the current calendar date)
Ensure Phone Number is present (prefilled) or valid
test_cases.expected_result: FAIL — Submission blocked; inline validation shown indicating the Preferred Date is invalid per rule 'date must be at least the next business day'.

test_cases.test_case: Message Body contains only whitespace (should FAIL)
preconditions: User is on Support Center page
steps: Leave Subject and Category as desired (Subject optional)
In the Message Body (rich text) enter only whitespace characters and line breaks (e.g., spaces + newlines) and no other content
test_cases.expected_result: FAIL — Submission blocked; inline validation shown indicating 'message body must be present' (message body is treated as empty).

test_cases.test_case: Message Body contains only an embedded image (no text) (unclear content-type presence) — verify acceptance
preconditions: User is on Support Center page
Image available to embed/upload into rich text editor
steps: In the Message Body (rich text) insert/embed a single image or paste an inline image and do NOT add any textual characters
test_cases.expected_result: EXPECTED BEHAVIOR — Because Message Body is a rich_text field and the system 'ensures a message body is present', the form should treat embedded rich content as a present message body and accept the submission; UI shows 'Message sent successfully.' with a ticket ID. If the implementation requires textual characters, an inline validation error 'message body must be present' is shown. (Test verifies which behavior the system implements.)

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click 'Send Message' to check duplicate ticket creation
preconditions: User is on Support Center page
All required fields filled with valid data (Message Body present)
steps: Open Secure Message form and fill Subject/Category/Message Body (valid content)
Rapidly click or double-click the 'Send Message' button twice in quick succession
test_cases.expected_result: EXPECTED BEHAVIOR — Only a single successful submission result should be shown (one 'Message sent successfully.' message and one ticket ID). The UI should not display two success messages or two different ticket IDs. If duplicate submissions occur, two success messages or multiple ticket IDs will be visible.

test_cases.test_case: Preferred Date set to next calendar day when that day is not a business day (weekend) — should FAIL
preconditions: User is on Support Center page
Test executed on a day where tomorrow is a weekend day (execute on a Friday so tomorrow is Saturday)
steps: Set Preferred Date to the next calendar day which is not a business day (e.g., if today is Friday, set date to Saturday)
test_cases.expected_result: FAIL — Submission blocked; inline validation shown indicating the Preferred Date is invalid per rule 'date must be at least the next business day' because the selected date is not the next business day.
